zh.lifeSummary: 當Margaret Langford於1438年出生在英國波克夏布拉德費爾德時，她的父親Edward de Langford22歲，母親Sancha le Blount23歲。她於1454年之前與Nicholas Carew, Esq. III結婚。他們至少是1個兒子和3個女兒的父母。她去世於1501年在英國素瑞貝丁頓享年63歲。

nameMeanings.name: Langford
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: habitational name from any of several places called with Old English lang ‘long’ + ford ‘ford’, such as Langford (Bedfordshire, Nottinghamshire, Essex, Norfolk).

variants.name: Lankford
meanings: English: apparently a variant of   Langford  .

variants.name: Langfitt
meanings: Probably an altered form of English   Langford  .

nameMeanings.name: Margaret
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

variants.name: Margareta
meanings: From the Latin form of Greek Margarītēs. See Margaret .

variants.name: Margaretta
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

historicEvents.title: Wars of the Roses
historicEvents.description: Wars of the Roses was a series of English civil wars for control of the throne of England between the royal families House of Lancaster and the House of York. The name of the war was derived as the red rose was the symbol of the Lancastrians and the white rose was the symbol of the Yorks.

en.lifeSummary: When Margaret Langford was born in 1438, in Bradfield, Berkshire, England, her father, Edward de Langford, was 22 and her mother, Sancha le Blount, was 23. She married Nicholas Carew, Esq. III before 1454. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 3 daughters. She died in 1501, in Beddington, Surrey, England, at the age of 63.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Margaret Langford est née en 1438, à Bradfield, Berkshire, Angleterre, son père, Edward de Langford, avait 22 ans et sa mère, Sancha le Blount, avait 23 ans. Elle a épousé Nicholas Carew, Esq. III avant 1454. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils et 3 filles. Elle est décédée en 1501, à Beddington, Surrey, Angleterre, à l’âge de 63 ans.

ko.lifeSummary: Margaret Langford(이)가 1438년 영국 버크실르 브래드필드에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Edward de Langford(은)는 22세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Sancha le Blount(은)는 23세였다. 그녀는 1454년 이전 Nicholas Carew, Esq. III(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1501년 영국 수리 베딩턴에서 63세의 나이로 사망했다.
